title: Standard Cornmeal Fly Food: 2 Liter Batch
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ujpok","text":"Directions for making standard cornmeal fly food in the Stevison Lab. Two liter batch yields approximately 300 vials with ~7.5 mL food per vial. Optional steps include making the fly food with 0.1% v/v blue food coloring and addition of 0.2 g/mL yeast paste with 0.2% v/v blue food coloring. This recipe is modified from the standard Bloomington Stock Center Recipe (Bloomington Drosophila Stock Center","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":365,"length":37}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7mbq","text":"Cornmeal Food Recipe).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

g","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Unwrap 3 trays of vials and label trays with your initials and date
Assemble pump, set dispensing time to 1 sec with 175 mL/min flow
Use the graduated pitcher to add 2 liters of diH2O to AROMA rice cooker
Cover with lid and then press 'On/Off' then 'Mode' to boil (~25 minutes)
Combine solid ingredients into 1.5 liter Tupperware, cover and shake to mix
Measure out 118 mL of 1 M propionic acid into 600 mL beaker
Measure 44 mL EtOH in 100 mL graduated cylinder add to 250 mL beaker
Measure 4.4 g of Tegosept mix into 44 mL EtOH in the 250 mL beaker
Whisk solid ingredients into boiling water, bring back to boil, stir continuously
Press 'Mode' to switch rice cooker to 'Warm' and continue stirring
Optional: Add food coloring and stir until homogeneous
At 70°C (158°F) whisk propionic acid and tegosept solutions into the food
Use pH strips to measure food ready to dispense (should be pH 4 - 5)
Begin dispensing food into empty vials, remix food before starting next tray
Wrap completed tray in two layers of cheesecloth, allow to dry for 24 hours
In 50 mL beaker add 4.0 g of live yeast, 20 mL of diH2O, and 40 μL of food coloring
After stirring, dispense 50 μL of yeast paste into each vial, allow to dry for 24 hours
creator: Laurie Stevison
